headline: Spinout Exergy3 raises £10m to decarbonise industrial heat
summary: University of Edinburgh spinout Exergy3, which converts surplus renewable electricity into high-temperature industrial heat, raised £10 million in seed funding led by Axeleo Capital's Article 9 Green Tech Industry I fund, with Bayern Capital, Kibo Invest, Scottish Enterprise, Zero Carbon Capital and Old College Capital also participating.
raw_snippet: The round was led by Axeleo Capital, whose Article 9 Green Tech Industry I fund backs European technology startups driving the ecological transition of industry, alongside Bavaria-based public venture capital investor Bayern Capital and Singapore-based Kibo Invest. Existing investors Scottish Enterprise, Zero Carbon Capital and Old College Capital, the University of Edinburgh's in-house venture investment fund, also joined the round.

headline: Cnuic secures $3m pre-seed to unlock next-generation photonic chip production
summary: Edinburgh-based Cnuic raised $3m in an oversubscribed pre-seed round led by Tensor Ventures to develop a new photolithography device that uses light rather than electrons to enable fast, reconfigurable production of photonic chips. The technology is intended to democratise access to photonic chip manufacturing, with potential implications for European semiconductor sovereignty. Co-founder Omar Durrani described it as 'building the tools that make that possible at scale.'
raw_snippet: Cnuic, headquartered in Edinburgh, raised $3m in pre-seed funding led by Tensor Ventures and Blank Space Ventures, with Silicon Roundabout Ventures, Phasechange, SANDS, and Superlative also participating. The company has built a working prototype of a new photolithography device using light for fast, reconfigurable chip production. Co-founder Omar Durrani: 'We learned to use electrons. Now we are learning to use light.' Tensor Ventures' Martin Drdúl: 'This is a completely new technology and a major breakthrough.' Announced April 28, 2026.

headline: Scottish quantum hardware company raises £2.5m to build UK's sovereign manufacturing capability
summary: Glasgow-based Quantcore, a University of Glasgow spin-out, raised £2.5m in seed funding co-led by PXN Ventures, Blackfinch Ventures and Scottish Enterprise, with Quantum Exponential and STAC also participating. Quantcore plans to grow from four to 12 employees over 18 months and is the only UK company producing quantum hardware using niobium.
raw_snippet: Quantcore, a University of Glasgow spin-out, has raised £2.5m in seed funding to create a sovereign supply chain for quantum hardware, as the UK races to build domestic capacity in technologies critical to national security and economic competitiveness. The round was co-led by PXN Ventures, Blackfinch Ventures and Scottish Enterprise, with investment also coming from Quantum Exponential and STAC.

headline: Edinburgh Uni spin-out at cutting edge of cancer research gets £1.6m injection
summary: BIOCAPTIVA, a Scottish biotech firm behind a platform to revolutionise early diagnosis of difficult-to-detect cancers, raised £1.6 million in a fresh funding round and launched its first product in the US. The round was led by existing investor Archangels, with support from Old College Capital, BBI, Scottish Enterprise and new investor EverQuest Capital Partners. BIOCAPTIVA's magnetic bead technology addresses a bottleneck in liquid biopsy sample preparation.
raw_snippet: A Scottish biotech firm behind a platform that could revolutionise the early diagnosis and monitoring of difficult-to-detect cancers has raised £1.6 million in a fresh funding round and launched its first product in the US. Its latest financing round was led by existing investor Archangels, with support from Old College Capital, BBI, Scottish Enterprise and new investor EverQuest Capital Partners.

source_name: DuckDuckGo — Scottish VC search (via Daily Business)
headline: SWURF raises £200k to expand platform
summary: Edinburgh-based SWURF, a remote-working platform connecting workers to on-demand private meeting pods, raised £200k in March 2026 from existing angel backers including Skyscanner co-founder Gareth Williams, alongside new unnamed high-net-worth investors. The funding will accelerate rollout of SWURF Pods across Edinburgh with the goal of making Edinburgh the world's most flexible working-friendly city.
raw_snippet: SWURF secured £200,000 of investment on March 9, 2026, to accelerate expansion. Existing backers include Gareth Williams (Skyscanner co-founder) and Anna Lagerqvist Christopherson (owner of Boda BV, Green Room and Victoria Bar). New investors are unnamed high-net-worth individuals. Platform currently lists over 450 venues and serves approximately 14,000 users. Board chaired by Alison Grieve (G-Hold founder).

investors: Maven Capital Partners (Investment Fund for Scotland)
headline: Ultrasonic surgery specialist Nami appoints Nikki Palfrey as CEO and completes latest investment round
summary: Nami Surgical, a University of Glasgow spinout developing ultrasonic robotic-assisted surgery technology, completed a £1.9m round led by Eos Advisory with participation from Maven and Scottish Enterprise. The funding supports the company's transition from advanced development to commercialisation in the robotic-assisted surgery market. New CEO Nikki Palfrey was appointed alongside the close.
raw_snippet: Nami recently completed its latest investment, a £1.9 million round led by Eos Advisory with participation from the Investment Fund for Scotland managed by Maven Capital Partners (and delivered by the British Business Bank), and Scottish Enterprise. The funding supports the company's transition from advanced development to commercialisation as Nami targets the global robotic-assisted surgery market, which is forecast to reach over $25 billion by 2030.

headline: Maven invests £2.6 million in Esk
summary: Esk, a global entertainment technology company specialising in high-end live experiences, secured £2.6 million from Maven Income and Growth VCTs and IFS Maven Equity Finance. Esk licenses iconic IP from major rightsholders (Netflix, Paramount, BAFTA) and transforms it into live entertainment formats, and has developed a proprietary media control system for audio-visual playback in venues. The IFS Maven Equity Finance involvement indicates Scottish operations.
raw_snippet: Esk, global entertainment technology company specializing in delivering high-end live experiences. Licenses iconic intellectual property from major rightsholders (Netflix, Paramount, BAFTA) and transforms it into live entertainment formats. Funding Amount: £2.6 million from Maven Income and Growth VCTs and IFS Maven Equity Finance (managed by Maven, delivered by British Business Bank). Announcement Date: May 11, 2026.

headline: Healthtech firm Bioliberty to accelerate international growth with new investment
summary: Scottish healthtech firm Bioliberty secured £6.2m in a partial close of its Series A round, led by £3m from the Scottish National Investment Bank alongside Archangels, Eos Advisory, Old College Capital, Hanna Capital SEZC, and new investor Conduit Connect. The full Series A target is $10.5m (approximately £8m) with the round expected to close later in 2026. This follows a £1.5m pre-Series A round in mid-2025.
raw_snippet: Healthtech firm, Bioliberty, has secured a total of £7.7m of funding over the last 10 months. The most recent £6.2m Series A investment was led by £3m from the Scottish National Investment Bank, alongside participation from existing investors Archangels, Eos Advisory, Old College Capital, and Hanna Capital SEZC, as well as new investor Conduit Connect. The full Series A round is expected to close later this year.

source_name: DuckDuckGo — Scottish VC search (via ADBA press release)
headline: $3 Million Investment for Edinburgh-Based Specialist Bio-Carbon Designer Carbogenics
summary: Carbogenics, a University of Edinburgh spinout founded in 2016, closed a $3m funding round on January 19, 2026 to accelerate development and international expansion of its CreChar biochar product. Equity investors include Dangerous Ventures (US), Green Angel Ventures (UK), and Old College Capital; grant funding from Innovate UK, New Mexico Gas, and New Mexico Government Economic Development Department also contributed. The company plans US manufacturing expansion alongside scaled UK operations.
raw_snippet: Carbogenics closed $3 million in investment and grant funding on January 19, 2026. Equity investors: Dangerous Ventures, Green Angel Ventures, Old College Capital (University of Edinburgh venture fund), Scottish Enterprise. Grant bodies: Innovate UK, New Mexico Gas, New Mexico Government Economic Development Department. Funds accelerate development of CreChar biochar for the biogas industry and support US manufacturing expansion.

headline: GovTech startup ePass rolls out nationwide platform after £1m debut year
summary: Edinburgh-based ePass, founded in 2024 by James Buchan and Chris Renga following a CivTech competition win, achieved £1m revenue in its first twelve months and deployed its licensing platform across all 32 Scottish local authorities serving over 15,000 businesses. The company is actively engaging with VC and investor communities and is likely to pursue its first external fundraising round later in 2026. This is not yet a closed investment round.
raw_snippet: GovTech startup ePass generated £1m revenue in its debut year and rolled out its platform nationwide across Scotland's 32 local authorities. Co-founders James Buchan (CEO) and Chris Renga (CTO) note the company is 'actively engaging with venture capital and investor communities' and is 'likely to embark on its first external fundraising round later this year.' No external VC investment has yet been closed as of announcement date May 25, 2026.

source_name: DuckDuckGo — Scottish VC search (via Scottish Financial Review)
headline: EnteroBiotix, Bellshill biopharma, raises £19m
summary: EnteroBiotix, a Bellshill-based biopharmaceutical company focused on microbiome therapeutics, raised £19m in growth financing led by Thairm Bio, with participation from the Scottish National Investment Bank. The funds will advance lead candidate EBX-102-02 into Phase 2b development for irritable bowel syndrome with constipation, following a positive Phase 2a trial. First patient dosing is expected Q2 2026 with topline data anticipated H2 2027.
raw_snippet: EnteroBiotix secured £19m in growth financing led by Thairm Bio and the Scottish National Investment Bank. Following the positive Phase 2a TrIuMPH trial outcome demonstrating efficacy of EBX-102-02 over placebo in IBS patients, the company will commence the largest ever full-spectrum microbiome therapeutic trial in IBS. CEO James McIlroy confirmed first patient dosing expected Q2 2026 and topline efficacy data anticipated H2 2027. Announced April 29, 2026.

source_name: DuckDuckGo — Scottish VC search (via Scottish Financial Review)
headline: Entourage AI, Glasgow uni health spinout, in $5m raise
summary: Entourage AI, a University of Glasgow spinout focused on longevity science and AI-powered health data analysis, raised $5m in a pre-seed round led by Provision. The company opened a Research and Operations Centre at Glasgow's West of Scotland Science Park, with Scottish Government Business Minister Richard Lochhead attending. The company was founded by Professor Patrick T. Gunning and aims to transform how human healthspan is measured and extended.
raw_snippet: Entourage AI secured $5 million in pre-seed funding led by Provision, with participation from GU Holdings Ltd (University of Glasgow's investment arm), David Peterson, Brad Peltz, and angel investors. The University of Glasgow spinout opened a Research and Operations Centre in Glasgow's West of Scotland Science Park. Founded by serial entrepreneur Professor Patrick T. Gunning, the company focuses on longevity science using AI to transform how human health is measured. Announced March 18, 2026.

source_name: DuckDuckGo — Scottish VC search (via Glasgow City of Science and Innovation)
headline: Glasgow healthtech spinout raises £1.6m to transform cancer diagnosis with AI
summary: TileBio, a University of Glasgow spinout, raised £1.6m in seed funding led by Twin Path Ventures to commercialise AI technology capable of learning the 'language of cancer' from unlabelled tissue images. The company's self-learning models bypass the need for manual data annotation — a key bottleneck in digital pathology AI — and will use the funding to accelerate commercialisation and clinical deployment of its diagnostic platform.
raw_snippet: TileBio secured £1.6m in seed funding led by Twin Path Ventures, with participation from Scottish Enterprise and GU Holdings Ltd. CEO Dr Christopher Walsh: 'We have developed a method that allows AI systems to interpret the language of tissue directly from millions of unlabelled images.' Twin Path Ventures' Katie Lockwood: 'Their models completely bypass the industry's biggest bottleneck: the need for manual data annotation.' Announced March 3, 2026.

headline: Edinburgh AI ecommerce firm raises over £1m in new investment
summary: Edinburgh-based stampfree.ai, which develops AI-powered parcel shipping and returns technology, raised just over £1m in a new round led by long-term backer R42 Group alongside crowdfunding investors on Crowdcube and Republic. The company's innovations include WhatsApp-based returns management and patented label-free shipping technology deployed with Royal Mail, Evri, and QazPost in Kazakhstan. CEO Hugh Craigie Halkett described the round as oversubscribed.
raw_snippet: stampfree.ai raised £1,029,968 in a new investment round as of May 20, 2026. R42 Group is described as a 'long-term investor' providing 'continued backing' across all fundraising rounds. Additional investors include the Crowdcube and Republic crowdfunding communities plus unnamed existing and new investors. CEO Hugh Craigie Halkett: 'This latest investment round was once again hugely oversubscribed, reflecting the confidence both of existing and new investors.' The company has secured EU and US patents for its label-free technology.

headline: Tricapital: Scots syndicate leads £3m-plus deals and unveils key hire
summary: Scottish angel syndicate Tricapital backed subsea robotics specialist HonuWorx with £506,000 as part of a series of investments totalling more than £3 million, focused on the industrial transition and ocean technology.
raw_snippet: The latest investments involve £506,000 of backing for subsea robotics specialist HonuWorx, £430,000 for sustainable seaweed business Kaly and £620,000 for low-carbon insulation manufacturer Sisaltech.

headline: Tricapital: Scots syndicate leads £3m-plus deals and unveils key hire
summary: Scottish angel syndicate Tricapital backed sustainable seaweed business Kaly with £430,000 as part of a series of investments totalling more than £3 million.
raw_snippet: The latest investments involve £506,000 of backing for subsea robotics specialist HonuWorx, £430,000 for sustainable seaweed business Kaly and £620,000 for low-carbon insulation manufacturer Sisaltech.

headline: Tricapital: Scots syndicate leads £3m-plus deals and unveils key hire
summary: Scottish angel syndicate Tricapital backed low-carbon insulation manufacturer Sisaltech with £620,000 as part of a series of investments totalling more than £3 million.
raw_snippet: The latest investments involve £506,000 of backing for subsea robotics specialist HonuWorx, £430,000 for sustainable seaweed business Kaly and £620,000 for low-carbon insulation manufacturer Sisaltech.

headline: Lentitek raises £700k for cancer treatment technology
summary: Edinburgh biotech Lentitek secured £700,000 from Scottish angel syndicate Equity Gap to accelerate its next-generation cancer treatments, bringing total investment in the company to £1 million in six months following a £300,000 Innovate UK grant.
raw_snippet: Biotech company Lentitek has secured £700,000 from Equity Gap, the Scottish angel syndicate, to accelerate its next-generation cancer treatments. It brings total investment in the Edinburgh business to £1 million in the last six months, following a £300,000 Innovate UK grant.

headline: Launchpad announces PXN Ventures as its newest strategic investor
summary: Edinburgh-based Launchpad, an AI-first robotics company for assembly automation, announced PXN Ventures as a new strategic investor in February 2026. The company previously announced an oversubscribed $11m Series A in October 2025, and the SNIB and Scottish Enterprise are also backers.
raw_snippet: Launchpad, the AI-first robotics company powering real-world assembly automation, today announced PXN Ventures as a new investor. This builds on the company's oversubscribed, $11M Series A fundraising, announced in October 2025.

headline: Edinburgh cyber security start up raises millions in pre-seed round
summary: Edinburgh-founded Infrawatch, a cyber-threat infrastructure intelligence platform built by CrowdStrike/Recorded Future/Intel 471 alumni, raised an undisclosed multi-million-pound pre-seed round led by Outward VC, with Triple Point Ventures, Portfolio Ventures and unnamed angel investors participating. Announced approximately May 2026 (exact date not stated).
raw_snippet: This funding round was led by Outward VC along with Triple Point Ventures and Portfolio Ventures and leading fintech and cyber angel investors.

headline: Go Swag raises $5m seed round led by Mercia Ventures
summary: Glasgow-based Go Swag, a tech-powered platform modernising corporate gifting, raised $5 million in seed funding led by Mercia Ventures with participation from Techstart Ventures and strategic angel investors. The round brings total funding to $6.2 million. Go Swag supplies merchandise to major companies including Meta, Apple, Netflix and ElevenLabs, serving over 1,000 companies in total.
raw_snippet: Go Swag secured $5 million (approximately £3.7 million) in a seed funding round announced on May 27, 2026. The funding round was led by Mercia Ventures, with participation from Techstart Ventures and strategic angel investors. This brought the company's total funding to $6.2 million. Go Swag is a Glasgow-based ecommerce startup providing a tech-powered platform modernising corporate gifting.

headline: Maven invests £2.2m in Scotmas Group
summary: Maven Capital Partners invested £2.2m in Scotmas Group, a Scottish Borders-based company, in January 2026. Scotmas Group is referenced as a recent investment by Maven Capital Partners managing the Investment Fund for Scotland. No further detail is available from this source.
raw_snippet: Recent 2026 investments include Esk Film Services (£2.6M, Edinburgh), Benbecula Distillery (£1.5M), Scotmas Group (£2.2M, January 2026), and PowerPhotonic (£2.6M).

headline: Gigged.AI secures £1m of additional funding from Par Equity and Scottish Enterprise
summary: Glasgow-based Gigged.AI, an AI-powered platform for recruiting technical talent, has secured £1m of additional funding over a ten-month period, including new investment from Par Equity and Scottish Enterprise. The round also includes participation from high-profile angel investors.
raw_snippet: Glasgow, London – Gigged.AI has secured £1m of additional funding over the last 10 months including new investment from Par Equity and Scottish Enterprise. The round also has notable participation from high profile angel investors including

headline: Scottish biotech Bead BioPharma emerges from stealth mode with novel cancer drug platform
summary: Bead BioPharma, a Scottish life sciences company backed by Archangels, announced its emergence from stealth mode following the submission of its first patent, marking its formal entry into the oncology (antibody-drug conjugate) sector. No specific new funding round or amount was disclosed in this article.
raw_snippet: Archangels-backed company files first patent on platform targeting one of oncology's fastest-growing therapy classes. Scotland's Bead BioPharma, a Scottish life sciences company backed by Archangels, announced its emergence from stealth mode following the submission of its first patent, a milestone that marks the company's formal entry into one of the most commercially active sectors in global oncology.

headline: TRICAPITAL Angels leads £1.2m investment into sovereign dual use 5G solutions
summary: JET Connectivity, developer of land and sea-based alternatives to satellite networks like Starlink, secured a £1.2m Pre-Series A round led by TRICAPITAL Angels with participation from private investors and The FSE Group, to scale its secure sovereign 5G network systems.
raw_snippet: The £1.2m Pre-Series A funding round was led by TRICAPITAL Angels, with participation from private investors and The FSE Group.

headline: Maven invests £2.6 million in Esk
summary: Edinburgh-based Esk Film Services received a £2.6m investment from Maven Capital Partners, believed to be via the IFS Maven Equity Finance fund (Investment Fund for Scotland). The deal appeared as a recent headline on Maven's news page. No announcement date is available; confirmed as a 2026 investment from Maven's Scottish portfolio. The company provides film services to the creative industries sector.
raw_snippet: Maven invests £2.6 million in Esk [news headline on https://www.mavencp.com/latest-news, retrieved 2026-06-27]. Company confirmed as Edinburgh-based Esk Film Services in third-party investor directory.

headline: Earth Blox secures £6m to help businesses and banks finance the transition to a nature-positive economy
summary: Edinburgh-based Earth Blox secured £6 million led by PXN Ventures with Scottish Enterprise, follow-on from Archangels, and the European Space Agency. The company helps organisations understand how nature loss and climate hazards affect financial value. The investment will fund team growth and product development.
raw_snippet: Earth Blox, an Edinburgh-based technology company helping organisations understand how nature loss and climate hazards affect financial performance, has secured £6 million in new investment led by PXN Ventures with support from Scottish Enterprise, follow-on investment from Archangels and funding from the European Space Agency (ESA).

headline: IFS Maven Equity Finance invests £1.25 million in Highway Data Systems
summary: Glasgow-based Highway Data Systems (HDS) received £1.25 million from IFS Maven Equity Finance (Investment Fund for Scotland) in what represents the first investment through the Greater Glasgow Innovation Cluster. HDS develops automated quality assurance technologies for road construction and asphalt industries, combining proprietary hardware, embedded electronics and software. The funding supports new contract delivery, customer expansion, product development and US operations.
raw_snippet: Highway Data Systems (HDS), located in Glasgow, Scotland, develops automated quality assurance technologies for road construction and asphalt industries. Investment amount of £1.25 million from Investment Fund for Scotland managed by Maven, delivered by the British Business Bank. This represents the first investment through the Greater Glasgow Innovation Cluster.

headline: Strathclyde-supported start-up secures backing to develop lower-cost wind energy
summary: Regeno, a University of Strathclyde spinout based in the Glasgow City Innovation District, secured investment from SFC Capital (lead), Scottish Enterprise, One Planet Capital, Gabriel Investment Syndicate, British Business Bank and the University of Strathclyde. The company is developing an innovative wind turbine design that can be serviced from ground level, eliminating the need for large cranes and reducing installation costs. CEO Adarsh Bhardwaj is a Strathclyde alumnus who received early-stage support through Strathclyde Inspire.
raw_snippet: Regeno, based in the Glasgow City Innovation District, is developing innovative wind turbine technology designed to be serviced from ground level, eliminating the need for large cranes or offshore equipment. Investors: University of Strathclyde, SFC Capital (lead investor), Scottish Enterprise, One Planet Capital, Gabriel Investment Syndicate, British Business Bank. Announcement Date: 20 March 2026.

headline: VASO secures funding to 'accelerate' growth
summary: Dumfries-based construction technology business VASO Global has raised a £5m+ funding package to accelerate manufacture of low-carbon homes built from recycled glass. The package includes a £1.4m seed investment from PXN Ventures, £621,000 from Scottish Enterprise, a £2m Innovate UK Loan (UKRI), and £800,000 from Eco Group, to expand its modular housing technology and support up to 70 jobs at a new 60,000sq ft Dumfries facility.
raw_snippet: The funding package includes a £1.4 million seed investment from PXN Ventures, £621,000 from Scottish Enterprise, a £2 million loan from Innovate UK Loans Limited, part of UKRI, and £800,000 from Eco Group.

headline: BR-DGE raises £10m from Bettor Capital and Greyfriars
summary: Edinburgh fintech BR-DGE, a payments orchestration platform for enterprise merchants in gaming and online retail, raised £10m in a growth round from new investor Bettor Capital (a US gaming-focused specialist) with continued backing from long-term advisor Greyfriars. Funds will support international expansion and platform development; the round coincided with the appointment of Perry Blacher as chairman.
raw_snippet: Bettor Capital, a US-based specialist investor focused on the gaming industry, joined the round; Greyfriars continues to act as long-term investment advisor to the company.

headline: Congrats to Wordsmith for raising its $70m series B
summary: Edinburgh legal AI startup Wordsmith raised its $70m Series B, noted in the Campfire Scotland newsletter. Wordsmith builds the legal operations platform for in-house legal teams. The round was led by Highland Europe and Index Ventures, bringing total funding to approximately $100m.
raw_snippet: Here's your Campfire for Monday, June 8th. Congrats to Wordsmith for raising its $70m series B!

headline: Edinburgh Fintech Aveni Raises £12m for AI Assurance Platform
summary: Edinburgh-based Aveni, a UK AI fintech specialist for wealth management and banking compliance, raised £12 million led by PXN Ventures with support from existing investors Puma Growth Partners, Lloyds Banking Group, Nationwide and Scottish Enterprise. The funding backs its Unified Assurance Platform and new products assessing AI agent conduct risk in financial services.
raw_snippet: Edinburgh-based Aveni has raised £12 million in a funding round led by PXN Ventures to accelerate the development of its Unified Assurance Platform... The round was supported by existing investors Puma Growth Partners, Lloyds Banking Group, Nationwide and Scottish Enterprise.

source_name: DuckDuckGo — Scottish VC search (via BusinessCloud) + STAC (Smart Things Accelerator Centre) — News
headline: STAC backs three Scottish deeptech companies with £772k
summary: Glasgow-based Vuabl raised £222k from the STAC Invest syndicate as part of a £772k cohort investment in three companies completing STAC's 18-month accelerator programme. Vuabl has built software using the LIDAR sensors in modern smartphones to scan properties and generate comprehensive condition reports, supporting housing providers meeting new energy efficiency and tenant safety regulations.
raw_snippet: STAC deployed £772,000 across three Glasgow-based deeptech ventures: Quantcore Technologies (£250,000), Vuabl (£222,000), and Airspection (£300,000). All three completed STAC's 18-month accelerator programme. Vuabl uses smartphone LIDAR sensors for property condition reports and EPC ratings. Announced April 1, 2026.

source_name: DuckDuckGo — Scottish VC search (via BusinessCloud) + STAC (Smart Things Accelerator Centre) — News
headline: STAC backs three Scottish deeptech companies with £772k
summary: Glasgow-based Airspection raised £300k from the STAC Invest syndicate as part of a £772k cohort investment in three companies completing STAC's 18-month accelerator programme. Airspection builds autonomous drone technology for offshore wind turbine inspections, flying from safe locations to remote infrastructure in extreme conditions to enhance safety and reduce environmental impact.
raw_snippet: STAC deployed £772,000 across three Glasgow-based deeptech ventures: Quantcore Technologies (£250,000), Vuabl (£222,000), and Airspection (£300,000). All three completed STAC's 18-month accelerator programme. Airspection's drones fly to remote locations for offshore wind, power lines, rail networks, and coastal assets. Announced April 1, 2026.
